title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Inference Brief: Rabbitmq43 Upgrade Gate
summary: Tests mock amqplib method calls but never assert routing or failure behavior; deployment starts consumers before topology migration; a reconnect storm follows broker restart; rollback cannot read new message schemas. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rabbitmq43 upgrade gate increment in tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits.
Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Rabbitmq43 Upgrade Gate · Theory Rabbitmq43 Upgrade Gate
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9831 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Rabbitmq43 Upgrade Gate contract for the supplied data.
